commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes logger runtime resolution so blank `logging.file` falls back to the default log path, warns once on file append failures, and adds config/logging regression tests.

PR surface: Source +15, Tests +108. Total +123 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main passes `cfg?.file` through directly and drops false `appendLogLine` returns. The PR body also supplies copied Node live output for the after-fix blank fallback and path-preservation behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can add the missing logging test cleanup hook without changing product behavior.

target.title: fix(logging): surface silent file-write failures and reject empty paths
target.body:
## Summary

When `logging.file` resolves to a path that cannot be written to (empty string, whitespace-only, directory, read-only filesystem, etc.), `appendLogLine` swallows the error and returns `false`, and the caller used to drop the boolean return — so the gateway kept running while no log lines were ever persisted. This is inconsistent with `rotateLogFile`, which at least emits one stderr warning on failure.

Three changes (compatibility-preserving):

1. **resolveSettings** (`src/logging/logger.ts`): blank/whitespace-only `logging.file` falls back to the default rolling path (same as unset). **Non-empty configured paths are preserved verbatim** — trim is used only to detect blank values, never to rewrite a path, so a legitimate path with leading/trailing spaces keeps writing to the same file after upgrade.
2. **Transport** (`src/logging/logger.ts`): emit a one-time stderr warning (`[openclaw] log file write failed; continuing without file log ...`) on persistent write failure, mirroring the existing `rotateLogFile` failure path. The flag resets on the next successful write so fixing the path clears the warning.
3. **Schema** (`src/config/zod-schema.ts`): unchanged — `logging.file` stays an optional string. Blank values are handled at runtime resolution, so previously-stored empty paths do not break config validation/startup.

## Linked context

N/A (no existing issue)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `logging.file` with empty/whitespace paths silently dropped all log writes with zero feedback; an earlier draft trimmed non-empty paths, which would silently redirect logs to a different file.
- **Real environment tested:** Node 24.14, Linux x86_64, local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` at `08ec9ad7bb8` on branch `fix/logging-silent-file-write-failure`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  node --import tsx /tmp/verify-logging-file.mjs
  ```
  Imports the patched logger via the `logging` barrel and verifies blank fallback, exact-byte preservation, and plain-path handling.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture of `node` runtime verification (copied live output):

  ```
  === logging.file resolution (resolveSettings via setLoggerOverride) ===
  [PASS] empty falls back to default -> file=/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-06-23.log
  [PASS] whitespace-only falls back to default -> file=/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-06-23.log
  [PASS] tab-only falls back to default -> file=/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-06-23.log
  [PASS] leading+trailing spaces preserves exact bytes -> got="  /tmp/verify-openclaw-keep.log  "
  [PASS] plain path preserves exact bytes -> got="/tmp/verify-openclaw-plain.log"

  ALL PASS
  ```

  `/tmp/openclaw/...` is the resolved default rolling log path for this environment. Non-empty paths with surrounding spaces resolve to the exact configured string (not trimmed).

- **Observed result after fix:** Blank/whitespace `logging.file` falls back to the default rolling path instead of passing `""` to `fs.appendFileSync`. Write failures emit a one-time stderr warning. Non-empty paths — including those with leading/trailing spaces — keep their exact bytes (no silent trim).
- **What was not tested:** End-to-end with a real read-only container filesystem (covered by the chmod-based write-failure test).

## Tests and validation

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/log-file-silent-failure.test.ts src/config/logging-file-empty.test.ts src/logging/log-file-size-cap.test.ts src/logging/logger-settings.test.ts
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests       14 passed (14)
```

## Risk checklist

- [x] User-visible behavior? **Yes** — blank/whitespace `logging.file` now falls back to the default rolling path instead of silent write failures; unwritable paths emit a one-time stderr warning.
- [x] Configuration? **No schema change** — `logging.file` stays an optional string; blank values are handled at runtime resolution. Non-empty paths (incl. surrounding spaces) are preserved verbatim, so no existing configured path is silently rewritten.
- [x] Security? **No**
- [x] Plugins/providers? **No**
- [x] Docs? **No**
- [x] Backwards-compatible? **Yes** — all previously-valid non-empty paths work identically with bytes preserved; blank paths that previously silently failed now fall back to default or warn.
target.author: miorbnli

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
